mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals imminent labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term earnings momentum and drives S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases (hit rate < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.5% MoM are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer confidence and corporate hiring, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that reinforces positive market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth surprises fail to predict 5-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) across multiple data vintages.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and reduced corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances.
